intro: Nouns are naming words!
Common nouns name general things like boy, city, or puppy.
Proper nouns name special people or places like Advaita, India, or Simba!
concept.tip: Proper nouns always start with a Capital letter!

problems.q: Which of these is a Proper Noun (a special name that starts with a capital letter)?
problems.why: Asoka is the special name of a famous emperor!

problems.q: Which of these is a Proper Noun naming a person or place?
problems.why: Sita is a proper noun naming a person!

problems.q: Which of these is a Common Noun (a general name for a person, place, or thing)?
problems.why: City is a general name, while Kolkata and India are proper nouns!

intro: Nouns can name males or females!
For example: actor and actress, emperor and empress.
Learning gender nouns helps us talk about people accurately!
concept.tip: Look at endings like -ess for many feminine forms!

problems.q: What is the feminine gender for 'actor'?
problems.why: An actor is male, and an actress is female!

problems.q: What is the feminine gender for 'emperor'?
problems.why: An emperor rules an empire, and an empress is a female ruler!

problems.q: What is the feminine gender for 'duke'?
problems.why: A duke and a duchess are noble titles!

intro: A collective noun names a whole group of people, animals, or things!
We say a flock of birds, a herd of cows, and a pride of lions.
One special word names many together!
concept.tip: Think of a team of players or a bunch of grapes!

problems.q: Which collective noun represents a group of ships or similar items?
problems.why: 'ships' is a collective noun naming a group!

problems.q: Which collective noun represents a group of soldiers or similar items?
problems.why: 'soldiers' is a collective noun naming a group!

problems.q: Which collective noun represents a group of cattle or similar items?
problems.why: 'cattle' is a collective noun naming a group!

problems.q: Which collective noun represents a group of players or similar items?
problems.why: 'players' is a collective noun naming a group!

problems.q: Which collective noun represents a group of people or similar items?
problems.why: 'people' is a collective noun naming a group!

problems.q: Which collective noun represents a group of sheep or similar items?
problems.why: 'sheep' is a collective noun naming a group!

problems.q: Which collective noun represents a group of pupils or similar items?
problems.why: 'pupils' is a collective noun naming a group!

problems.q: Which collective noun represents a group of judges or similar items?
problems.why: 'judges' is a collective noun naming a group!

intro: Abstract nouns name ideas, feelings, and qualities!
You cannot touch or see bravery, honesty, or wisdom, but you can feel and show them.
They are the most powerful words in our minds!
concept.tip: If it is a feeling or idea like kindness or joy, it is an abstract noun!

problems.q: Which of these is an Abstract Noun (a quality or feeling you cannot touch)?
problems.why: Bravery is a noble quality inside your heart!

problems.q: Which of these is an Abstract Noun representing truthful character?
problems.why: Honesty means always telling the truth!

problems.q: Which of these is an Abstract Noun representing joy and sound?
problems.why: Laughter is a wonderful feeling of happiness!

problems.q: What does 'Egoist' mean? (Someone who is supremely selfish)
What does 'Egotist' mean? (Someone who is boastful and conceited)

problems.why: Someone who considers themselves the center of the universe

problems.q: What does 'Egomaniac' mean? (Someone obsessed with their own desires/needs to a dangerous degree)
problems.why: Someone obsessed with their own desires/needs to a dangerous degree

problems.q: What does 'Altruist' mean? (Someone who is more interested in the welfare of others than their own)
problems.why: Someone who is more interested in the welfare of others than their own

problems.q: What does 'Alter ego' mean? (A secondary personality or a very close friend who is a mirror image)
problems.why: A secondary personality or a very close friend who is a mirror image

problems.q: What does 'Altercation' mean? (A strong verbal dispute)
What does 'Introvert' mean? (Someone whose interests are turned inwards)
problems.why: Someone whose interests are turned inwards

problems.q: What does 'Extrovert' mean? (Someone whose interests are turned outwards)
problems.why: Someone whose interests are turned outwards

problems.q: What does 'Ambivert' mean? (Someone who has both introverted and extroverted tendencies)
problems.why: Someone who has both introverted and extroverted tendencies

problems.q: What does 'Dexterous' mean? (Skillful with the hands)
What does 'Ambidextrous' mean? (Able to use both hands with equal skill)
problems.why: Able to use both hands with equal skill

problems.q: What does 'Adroit' mean? (Skillful or clever)
What does 'Gauche' mean? (Awkward or clumsy)
What does 'Misanthrope' mean? (Someone who hates or distrusts humankind)
problems.why: Someone who hates or distrusts humankind

problems.q: What does 'Misogynist' mean? (Someone who hates women)
What does 'Misogamist' mean? (Someone who hates marriage)
What does 'Philanthropist' mean? (Someone who loves and helps humankind)
problems.why: Someone who loves and helps humankind

problems.q: What does 'Monogamist' mean? (One married to one person)
What does 'Bigamist' mean? (One married to two people)
What does 'Polygamist' mean? (One married to many people)
